questions.explanation: The Android SDK provides the APIs, build tools, emulator, and debugging utilities needed to develop Android applications.

options.A: A set of tools and APIs used to build, test, and debug Android apps
options.B: A cloud database service for storing user data
options.C: A programming language used to write Android apps
options.D: A UI design tool used only for creating icons

questions.question: In Android development, what is the primary role of the Android SDK?

questions.explanation: Kotlin is officially supported by Google for Android development and is widely used alongside (and often instead of) Java.
questions.question: Which language is officially supported by Google as a first-class language for Android app development?

questions.explanation: Gradle is the build system used by Android projects to compile code, manage dependencies, and configure build types/flavors.

options.A: Building the app, managing dependencies, and defining build variants
options.D: Replacing the Android operating system on a device

questions.question: What is Gradle primarily used for in Android projects?

questions.explanation: An API (Application Programming Interface) defines how different software components interact, often via functions or network endpoints.

options.A: A set of rules/endpoints that allow software components to communicate
options.B: A type of mobile operating system kernel
options.C: A database table used for caching

questions.question: In programming, what does an "API" most commonly refer to?

questions.explanation: `remember` stores a value in the composition so it is not recreated on every recomposition (within the same composition lifecycle).
questions.question: In Jetpack Compose, which concept is used to hold UI state that should survive recomposition within the same composition?

questions.explanation: Retrofit relies on converter libraries (like Moshi or Gson) to deserialize JSON into strongly-typed model classes.

options.A: Add a converter (e.g., Moshi or Gson) and define data models
options.B: Use Room annotations on network DTOs to parse JSON automatically
options.C: Use Gradle flavors to transform JSON into objects at build time
options.D: Use Firebase Authentication to decode JSON responses

questions.question: When using Retrofit in Android, which approach is commonly used to parse JSON responses into Kotlin/Java objects?

questions.explanation: A DAO declares the database operations (SQL queries and CRUD methods) that Room implements to access the underlying SQLite database.

options.A: To define methods for querying, inserting, updating, and deleting data
options.B: To render database rows directly into UI components
options.C: To manage Gradle dependencies for database libraries
options.D: To authenticate users before database access

questions.question: In Room Database, what is the main purpose of a DAO (Data Access Object)?

questions.explanation: Retrofit handles network calls, Coroutines provide non-blocking async execution, and Room provides local persistence; a repository pattern commonly coordinates these layers.

options.A: Retrofit + Coroutines + Room, using a repository to coordinate network and cache
options.B: Room only, because it can automatically call REST endpoints
options.C: Firebase only, because it replaces all REST APIs and local storage
options.D: Gradle + Jetpack Compose, because build scripts can run network calls

questions.question: You need to fetch data from a REST API and store it locally. To avoid blocking the UI thread, which combination is most appropriate in a modern Android app?

questions.explanation: Side-effects like network calls should be launched from LaunchedEffect (or a ViewModel) so they run predictably and not on every recomposition.

options.A: Move the call into a side-effect such as LaunchedEffect (or a ViewModel) keyed appropriately
options.B: Wrap the network call in a try/catch and keep it in the composable body
options.C: Disable recomposition for the entire screen
options.D: Convert the network call into a Gradle task

questions.question: A Compose screen triggers a network request directly inside a composable body, causing repeated calls on recomposition. What is the best fix?

questions.explanation: Logic programming (e.g., Prolog-style) focuses on declaring facts/rules and letting an inference engine derive results from queries.

options.A: A paradigm where you declare facts and rules, and the system infers answers via queries
options.B: A paradigm where you only write UI code and the framework generates business logic
options.C: A build configuration approach used by Gradle to resolve dependencies
options.D: A mobile operating system feature that schedules background tasks

questions.question: Which statement best describes logic programming in the context of "use logic programming"?
